name: Fixed-Wing Drone (simpleFoam)
description: External aerodynamics for a small fixed-wing UAV/drone in freestream flow. Uses snappyHexMesh for body-fitted meshing and simpleFoam for steady-state RANS simulation. No ground plane — the geometry floats in open air with slip conditions on all domain boundaries. Sized for small drone scale (~0.8 m wingspan) at cruise speed of 12 m/s.
learning_objectives: Copy and prepare surface geometry (STL/OBJ format)
Refine mesh around geometry with snappyHexMesh
Configure freestream boundary conditions (slip on all domain faces)
Post-process lift and drag coefficients with forceCoeffs

mesh.title: Generate Mesh
mesh.description: Three-step meshing: surfaceFeatureExtract detects sharp edges, blockMesh creates the background hex mesh, then snappyHexMesh refines around the drone geometry. The domain should extend far enough in all directions to avoid blockage effects.
mesh.tip: For a small drone, the domain should extend at least 10 chord lengths upstream, 20 downstream, and 10 in all lateral directions to minimise far-field boundary influence.

boundaries.title: Set Boundary Conditions
boundaries.description: Configure freestream boundary conditions. All domain faces (top, bottom, front, back) use slip — there is no ground plane. Only the drone surfaces use no-slip wall conditions.
boundaries.tip: Unlike ground vehicle cases, lowerWall uses slip (not wall) because the drone is in freestream. The inlet fixes velocity at 12 m/s and the outlet uses inletOutlet.

solver.title: Configure Solver
solver.description: Set up simpleFoam with SIMPLE algorithm. This is a steady-state solver — endTime controls the maximum number of iterations.
solver.tip: For a quick test run, reduce endTime to 200 iterations. Full convergence at this low Reynolds number typically needs 500-1000 iterations.

run.title: Run Simulation
run.description: Execute simpleFoam. Watch residuals — all fields should decrease below 1e-4 for convergence. Monitor lift and drag coefficients for convergence.
run.tip: At 12 m/s the Reynolds number is lower than typical aircraft cases, so convergence may be smoother. Check both residuals and Cl/Cd convergence before stopping.

postprocess.title: View Results
postprocess.description: Visualise pressure distribution on the drone surface, velocity field around the wing, and wake structure. Check forceCoeffs output for lift and drag values (positive Cl = upward lift).
postprocess.tip: In ParaView: apply 'Extract Block' to select drone surfaces, then colour by pressure coefficient. Use a cutting plane at y=0 to visualise the symmetry plane flow field and check for separation.
